pattern: The transformation is a fixed permutation of positions, so the answer is the smallest power that returns it to identity, found by following one orbit back to its start.
hints: Each operation rearranges positions in the same deterministic way every time.
Track where a single index travels under repeated application; the number of steps to return it home is what you want.
Index 1 maps to n/2 under the inverse rule; simulate the position of element originally at index 1 until it comes back to 1, counting the operations.

edge_inputs.description: ten where orbit is not trivially small

stress.description: random even n across the valid range
